title: rosa coriifolia subsp. frutetorum
intro: Rosa coriifolia subsp. frutetorum, also known as the wild rose or desert rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa coriifolia plant, native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia subsp. frutetorum include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, as well as its potential to support cardiovascular health and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture, to utilize its medicinal properties.
